articles.title: These Never-Before-Seen Photos of Princess Diana Offer a Glimpse into Her Early Life
articles.content:
有些公众人物我们永远不会忘记，黛安娜王妃绝对是其中之一。她去世近三十年后，人们仍然对她在头条新闻和皇室戏剧背后真实的样子充满好奇。正因如此，一场新宣布的拍卖会已经引起广泛关注。一批从未公开的照片和个人通信即将拍卖，让人们难得地窥见黛安娜在成为威尔士王妃之前的漫长岁月。

时机也格外恰当。7月1日是黛安娜本应满65岁的生日，而7月29日则是她1981年与查尔斯王子婚礼的周年纪念日。如今，就在这两个日期之间，一位她童年好友的收藏让皇室粉丝有机会看到那个鲜为人知的少女时代的黛安娜。

那么，这批收藏具体包含什么？这些新公开的照片又揭示了这位将成为世界最知名人物之一的女性怎样的故事？继续阅读所有细节和照片。

这批收藏由英国戈林斯拍卖行出售，包含四张此前未公开的彩色照片，拍摄于黛安娜就读西希思女子学校的时期（1973年至1977年）。其中两张照片是黛安娜独自在卧室里，另一张拍摄于学校室外，最后一张则是她与一群朋友放松休息的场景。

这些照片来自黛安娜的长期好友凯瑟琳·汉伯里，她也曾就读于西希思学校。这些不是精心策划的皇室肖像或标志性的狗仔队照片，而是一位少女与朋友闲逛、过着出人意料地普通生活的快照。

除了照片，这批收藏还包含“与她的学生时代、友谊以及皇室蜜月相关的个人通信和纪念品”。其中最有趣的物品之一是黛安娜1981年从与查尔斯王子的蜜月归来后不久寄给汉伯里的一封三页手写信件。信写在白金汉宫信纸上，印有伊丽莎白二世女王的皇家印章，日期为1981年9月27日，也就是皇室婚礼后仅两个月，全球数亿人观看了那场婚礼。

在这封信中，黛安娜感谢朋友寄来的卡片，写道：“亲爱的凯瑟琳，我无法形容打开你的卡片时有多么惊喜。非常感谢你提笔写信祝福我。”原信封也包含在拍品中，使其成为一份特别私人的皇室历史文物。这批收藏还包括一张手写生日卡，署名“黛安娜(S)”。显然，这是同学们用来区分另一位同名学生的称呼。

也许最感人的物品是一份私人感恩礼拜程序，1997年11月19日在伦敦举行，黛安娜此前一年在巴黎车祸中去世。与在威斯敏斯特教堂举行的全球电视直播葬礼不同，这场私人追思会只有最亲近的人参加。因此，这类程序的存世数量极少，使其成为一件特别珍贵的纪念品。

整批收藏的估价在4600至6937美元之间——这可能比你想象的要低！但考虑到公众对黛安娜的持续关注，以及这些照片中有数张从未公开发布的事实，最终拍卖价格可能会大幅上涨。

是的，你没看错！拍卖会将于7月7日在苏塞克斯郡刘易斯的戈林斯拍卖行举行，作为其现场夏季艺术品与家居拍卖会的一部分。有意竞拍者可于6月9日前提交出价，无需亲自到场。

但请注意：最终成交价通常高于最终出价。拍卖行通常会收取买家佣金，根据竞拍者所在位置，可能还需支付额外费用或税费。

当然，并非每个人都准备好花费数千美元购买黛安娜纪念品。但即使对于那些从未打算竞拍的人，这批收藏也提供了一些有价值的东西：提醒人们记住那个在举世闻名之前的黛安娜。
articles.summary:
There are some public figures we just never stop thinking about, and Princess Diana is definitely one of them. Nearly three decades after her death, people are still fascinated by who she was behind the headlines and royal drama. That’s why a newly announced auction is already getting a ton of attention. A collection of never-before-seen photos and personal correspondence is about to go up for sale, offering a rare glimpse into Diana’s life long before she became the Princess of Wales.

The timing feels especially timely too. July 1 would have marked Diana’s 65th birthday, while July 29 marks the anniversary of her 1981 wedding to Prince Charles. And now, right in between those dates, a collection from one of her childhood friends is giving royal fans a chance to see the teenage Diana few people ever knew.

So what, exactly, is in the collection, and what do these newly revealed photos tell us about the woman who would become one of the most famous people in the world? Read on for all the details—and a look at the photos.

Get Reader’s Digest’s Read Up newsletter for more royals, holidays, fun facts, humor, cleaning, travel and tech all week long.

What do these photos reveal?

The collection, which is being sold by Gorringe’s auction house in the U.K., contains four previously unseen color photographs from Diana’s years at West Heath Girls’ School, where she attended from 1973 to 1977. Two of the photos show Diana alone in her bedroom, another captures her outside the school, and a fourth shows her relaxing with a group of friends.

The photos come from Diana’s longtime friend Katherine Hanbury, who also attended West Heath. And they aren’t carefully staged royal portraits or iconic paparazzi shots. They’re snapshots of a teenage girl hanging out with friends and living a life that looked surprisingly ordinary.

What other Diana memorabilia is being auctioned off?

In addition to the photos, the collection reveals “personal correspondence and ephemera, relating to her schooldays and friendships, and the Royal honeymoon,” according to Gorringe’s. One of the most interesting items in the collection is a three-page handwritten letter Diana sent to Hanbury shortly after returning from her honeymoon with Prince Charles in 1981. Written on Buckingham Palace stationery and bearing Queen Elizabeth II’s royal seal, the letter is dated Sept. 27, 1981, just two months after the royal wedding watched by millions around the world.

In the note, Diana thanked her friend for sending a card and wrote, “Dear Katherine, I can’t tell you what a lovely surprise I got opening your card. Thank you so much for putting pen to paper and wishing me well.” The original envelope is included as part of the lot, making it an especially personal piece of royal history. The collection also contains a handwritten birthday card signed “Diana (S).” Apparently, that was how classmates distinguished her from another student named Diana.

And then there’s perhaps the most emotional item of all: a program from a private Thanksgiving service held in London on Nov. 19, 1997, following Diana’s death in a car crash in Paris earlier that year. Unlike the globally televised funeral at Westminster Abbey, this intimate memorial service was attended by only those closest to her. As a result, very few copies of the program exist, making it a particularly rare keepsake.

How much is all of this expected to sell for?

The entire collection is expected to sell for between approximately $4,600 and $6,937—which is probably not as much as you might expect! But given the enduring interest in Diana and the fact that several of the photographs have never been publicly seen before, it wouldn’t be surprising if bidding ultimately pushes the final price higher.

Can you put in a bid too?

Yes, you can! The auction will take place at Gorringe’s auction house in Lewes, East Sussex, as part of their live Summer Fine Art & Interiors Sale on July 7. Interested buyers have until June 9 to enter their bid, and they aren’t required to be there in person.

But take note: The final cost is usually higher than the winning bid. Auction houses typically charge a buyer’s premium, and additional fees or taxes may apply depending on the bidder’s location.

Of course, not everyone has several thousand dollars ready to spend on Diana memorabilia. But even for those who never plan to bid, the collection offers something valuable: a reminder of the person Diana was before the world knew her name.

RELATED:

  • Here’s Why Princess Diana Thought Harry Would Make a Better King Than William
  • Princess Diana Once Had Her Own “Pretty Woman” Moment at a London Boutique—Here’s What Happened
  • Here’s Why Prince William and Prince Harry Won’t Inherit the Estate Where Princess Diana Is Buried

Why trust us

Reader’s Digest has published hundreds of stories on the British royal family, providing a behind-the-scenes look at the fascinating facets of the monarchy. We regularly cover topics including the latest royal news, the history and meaning behind time-honored traditions, and the everyday quirks of everyone’s favorite family members, from Queen Elizabeth’s daily snack to Prince William’s confessions about his home life. We’re committed to producing high-quality content by writers with expertise and experience in their field in consultation with relevant, qualified experts. We rely on reputable primary sources, including government and professional organizations and academic institutions as well as our writers’ personal experiences where appropriate. We verify all facts and data, back them with credible sourcing and revisit them over time to ensure they remain accurate and up to date. Read more about our team, our contributors and our editorial policies.

Sources:

  • Gorringe’s: “Summer Fine Art & Interiors Sale”
  • El País: “New Lady Di Auction: Unseen Photos and Letters from the Time Before She Was Princess of Wales”
  • Fine Books Magazine: “Princess Diana Private Correspondence and Rare Photographs to Auction”

The Best Thing William Learned from Diana

The One Thing Diana Was Not Allowed to Wear

Diana Knew Her Marriage Was Doomed

The post These Never-Before-Seen Photos of Princess Diana Offer a Glimpse into Her Early Life appeared first on Reader's Digest.

articles.title: Toy Story 5 Is Almost Here! Here’s a Cheat Sheet to Refresh Your Memory on the First 4 Movies Before Watching the New One
articles.content:
做好准备。 Woody、Buzz和安迪的玩具们第一次在银幕上亮相已经是三十年前的事了。是的，《玩具总动员》已经三十岁了！这部既搞笑又温馨感人的系列电影第五部将于6月19日上映。这部电影表面上讲的是一堆玩具在没人注意时会活过来，但《玩具总动员》系列实际上是对童年的美好致敬，充满了欢乐、心碎、担忧和失去。

我第一次看《玩具总动员》是1995年，和所有没有去看珍珠果酱演唱会或《独领风骚》放映会的人一样。（我说了，那是很久以前的事了。）如果你和我一样，已经记不清90年代、那部电影或之后的续集（以及几十年）的事了，那我们显然都需要一个回顾。接下来，我将带你回顾《玩具总动员》原版和全部三部续集——另外还会剧透一下最新一部的精彩片段——正好赶上《玩具总动员5》的上映。

在我研究的过程中，回忆如潮水般涌来——我打赌你也会如此。来吧，一起踏上这段回忆之旅！

**首映：1995年**

1995年的感恩节期间，《玩具总动员》登陆影院，成为一次震撼之作。这是第一部完全采用电脑生成图像（CGI）制作的长篇动画电影，《玩具总动员》开创了动画的新时代。但这部迪士尼/皮克斯电影的真正持久魅力在于，它以一个小男孩的玩具视角，甜美而幽默地呈现了童年的模样。

影片向我们介绍了牛仔警长胡迪——一个老式的、会说话的牛仔玩偶，拉动拉绳就能发声；以及Buzz Lightyear——一个电池驱动的太空 ranger玩具。他们为赢得即将满7岁的6岁男孩安迪的宠爱而争斗。胡迪由汤姆·汉克斯配音，他感到新来的Buzz（蒂姆·艾伦配音）对自己构成了威胁，担心自己不再是安迪的最爱，也不再是玩具们的领袖。这些玩具包括土豆先生、弹簧狗、霸王龙Rex（一只胆小的恐龙）、小猪存钱罐Hamm、牧羊女Bo Peep，还有一队绿色小 Army士兵。

故事情节围绕安迪的生日派对和搬家展开，这奠定了该系列的一个贯穿始终的主题：变化的必然性，以及孩子成长过程中对失去的普遍感受。但在第一部影片中，整体氛围还是比较轻松的，胡迪和Buzz互相斗嘴，在一家披萨店里迷路，最终意识到安迪仍然爱着他们。

**首映：1999年**

没有多少续集敢说与原作持平，更不用说超越原作了，但《玩具总动员2》做到了。胡迪和Buzz现在共享玩具们梦寐以求的联合领袖地位，但安迪正在长大，那些旧有的不安全感依然存在。胡迪的胳膊被扯破"受伤"后暂时被搁置一边，他开始担忧，因为架子上陪伴他的伙伴——一只不会发声的企鹅Wheezy——被放到了家庭旧货出售中。

在试图营救Wheezy的过程中，胡迪反而被人偷走，带到了一位玩具收藏家的公寓。在那里，他遇到了 Jessie the Cowgirl，一个被第一个也是唯一的主人抛弃的布偶。Jessie由琼·库萨克配音，她成为这部影片的核心人物，透过她的视角，我们看到了童年热爱的高低起伏。Jessie的故事线配上了莎拉·麦克拉克伦那首令人心碎的热门歌曲《When She Loved Me》，这首歌获得了奥斯卡提名并赢得了格莱美奖，所以如果你计划重温这部电影，最好准备一些纸巾。

玩具收藏家阴谋最终得到解决，胡迪和Jessie回到了安迪家。库萨克在后续续集中（包括《玩具总动员5》）继续回归配音，她饰演的这个活泼却心碎的牛仔女孩简直完美无缺，难怪这部电影如此闪耀、经得起时间的考验。令人难以置信的是，《玩具总动员2》最初是作为直接发行录像带的项目，后来才获得院线上映的批准——这是一件好事，因为它以9000万美元的预算获得了近5亿美元的票房。

**首映：2010年**

时间快进到2010年，安迪正准备去上大学。令胡迪高兴的是，安迪计划带着他这位忠诚的老牛仔伙伴一起走，尽管其他玩具注定要去阁楼。在一个对任何意外扔掉童年回忆的人来说都很熟悉的误会中，玩具袋阴差阳错地被放在了路边垃圾日。

为了避免一个绝对可怕的命运，玩具们设法把袋子挪到了捐赠箱里，胡迪也加入了他们，最终他们来到了一家日托中心。问题解决了！现在他们可以永远被玩耍了。当然，事情并非如此简单，因为安迪的玩具们（几乎）为时已晚地发现，这个中心的玩具领袖——Lotso-Huggin'-Bear（由已故的奈德·比蒂配音）——把这个地方管理得像一个劳改营。"面对现实吧，我们只是垃圾，"Lotso在关键一幕中说道，用这句话掩盖他被主人——一个叫Daisy的女孩——抛弃后的伤痛。

《玩具总动员3》充满了紧凑、剪辑精良的动作场面——同时没有牺牲前几部电影的任何核心和灵魂——成为票房和口碑的双重巨大成功。相比前两部电影，它在技术上也实现了重大飞跃，而且当玩具们面对"死亡"时，影片甚至有一点点可怕。

**首映：2019年**

又一部评论界和票房的双重巨大成功，《玩具总动员4》延续了上一部的故事。胡迪和玩具们现在属于邦妮——一个接受了安迪捐赠玩具的幼儿园小朋友——但他们正在努力融入现有的玩具等级制度。为了保护即将上学的紧张不安的邦妮，胡迪偷偷藏在她书包里跟着去学校，在那里遇到了Forky——一个邦妮用被丢弃的塑料餐具制作的玩具。

Forky因为自己是用垃圾做的而焦虑不安，反复试图把自己扔掉。胡迪惊恐不已，不断营救Forky，包括在一次家庭房车旅行中，两人遇到了胡迪的老朋友Bo Peep（由安妮·波莎配音）。原来Bo一直在作为"流浪玩具"四处游历，摆脱了被拥有的状态，而且对此非常满意。

大部分剧情围绕着回到房车展开（基努·里维斯作为70年代特技车玩具Duke Caboom出现帮忙），途中还经过了古董店和巡回嘉年华。胡迪（现在是Forky）的经典焦虑转化成了该系列迄今为止最高的票房收入，在全球获得超过10亿美元的票房。但影片改变了玩具需要被特定孩子拥有和爱护的叙事主题：最终，胡迪选择与Bo一起上路，放下了邦妮，也放下了与安迪的过去。

**首映：2026年**

对《玩具总动员5》的期待值很高，这实际上是该系列的第六部影片。（没看过2022年的《光年》衍生片？你不是一个人——遗憾的是，那部电影票房惨淡。）最新一部请回了汉克斯、艾伦和库萨克作为胡迪、Buzz和Jessie这个主角三人组，而且甚至还有泰勒·斯威夫特演唱的新歌《I Knew It, I Knew You》，将于6月19日首映前的6月5日发布。

故事设定是这样的：第一部《玩具总动员》已经过去30年，玩具和科技世界发生了很多变化。邦妮的新"玩具"登场了——Lilypad。由《晨间秀》的格蕾塔·李配音，Lilypad是一个现代平板电脑设备，外表看起来像一只无辜的青蛙，但正如官方预告片所示，它绝对有一个颠覆性的 agenda。

1995年时7岁的安迪现在可能已经有了自己的孩子，在我们等待上映的时候好好想想这一点吧。波莎和里维斯也将回归，还有约翰·拉岑贝格尔（Hamm）、华莱士·肖恩（Rex）和吉姆·瓦尼（Slinky Dog）。《玩具总动员5》将于6月19日在全国上映。我们迫不及待了。
articles.summary:
Brace yourselves. It’s been three decades since Woody, Buzz and the rest of Andy’s toys lit up the screen in the original Toy Story. Wait—Toy Story is 30? Yep, and the fifth outing of this hilarious and incredibly sweet series of movies will hit theaters on June 19. Ostensibly about a room full of toys that come to life when people aren’t looking, the Toy Story franchise is a beautiful, touching tribute to childhood, filled with joy, heartbreak, worry and loss.

I first saw Toy Story in 1995 like everybody else who wasn’t at a Pearl Jam concert or a screening of Clueless. (I told you, it was a long time ago.) If, like me, you’re having trouble remembering the ’90s, the movie or any of the subsequent sequels (and decades), it’s pretty clear we’re all going to need a recap. Ahead, I’ll take you through the original Toy Story and all three direct sequels—plus give you a bonus sneak peek of the newest one—just in time for the release of Toy Story 5.

As I researched, the memories came flooding back—and I bet they will for you too. So saddle up, and ride along!

Get Reader’s Digest’s Read Up newsletter for more movies, cleaning, humor, travel, tech and fun facts all week long.

Toy Story

Premiered: 1995

When Toy Story hit theaters just in time for Thanksgiving 1995, it was a revelation. The first feature-length movie made entirely in computer-generated imagery (CGI), Toy Story ushered in a new breed of animation. But the Disney/Pixar film’s real staying power is in its sweet, funny look at childhood through the eyes of a young boy’s toys.

The film introduces us to Sheriff Woody, an old-fashioned talking cowboy doll with a pull string, and Buzz Lightyear, a battery-operated space ranger toy, who battle for the affections of 6-turning-7-year-old Andy. Woody, voiced by Tom Hanks, feels threatened by newcomer Buzz (Tim Allen) and fears he will no longer be Andy’s favorite nor the leader of the toys, a menagerie of characters that includes Mr. Potato Head, Slinky Dog, Rex (a cowardly dinosaur), Hamm (a piggy bank), Bo Peep and a platoon of little green Army men.

The plot, centered around Andy’s birthday party and his family’s move, sets the stage for a running theme of the franchise: the inevitability of change and the universal feelings of loss as children grow up. But in this first movie, the vibe is mostly light, as Woody and Buzz trade barbs, get lost in a pizza parlor and ultimately realize that Andy still loves them.

Toy Story

Watch on Disney+

Watch on Amazon Prime Video

Toy Story 2

Premiered: 1999

Not many sequels can claim to be equal, let alone superior, to the original, but Toy Story 2 pulls it off. Woody and Buzz now share the toys’ coveted co-leader status, but Andy is growing up and the old insecurities remain. After Woody gets “injured” with a ripped arm, he is temporarily shelved and begins to fear the worst when his shelf companion, a squeaker-less penguin named Wheezy, is added to the family yard sale.

Attempting a rescue, Woody is instead stolen and brought to a toy collector’s apartment, where he meets Jessie the Cowgirl, a rag doll abandoned by her first and only owner. Jessie is voiced by Joan Cusack, and she becomes the heart of the film as we see the highs and lows of childhood devotion through her eyes. Jessie’s storyline is punctuated by a gut-wrenching Sarah McLaughlin banger, “When She Loved Me,” which was nominated for an Academy Award and won a Grammy, so you’re going to want some tissues handy if you plan a rewatch.

The resolution of the toy-collector plot finds Woody and Jessie back at Andy’s, and Cusack, who returns in later sequels (including Toy Story 5), is so pitch-perfect as the plucky yet heartbroken cowgirl that it’s no wonder this film shines so brightly and holds up so well. Incredibly, Toy Story 2 began life as a straight-to-video release before getting the green light to feature status—a good thing, since it grossed nearly $500 million on a $90 million budget.

Toy Story 2

Watch on Disney+

Watch on Amazon Prime Video

Toy Story 3

Premiered: 2010

Fast-forward to 2010, and Andy is getting ready to leave for college. To Woody’s delight, Andy plans to take his devoted old cowboy pal with him, though the rest of the toys are destined for the attic. In a mix-up that’s very familiar to anyone who has accidentally thrown away childhood memories, the toy bag instead ends up at the curb on trash day.

To prevent a decidedly awful fate, the toys manage to maneuver the bag over to a donation bin, where Woody joins them, and they wind up at a daycare center. Problem solved! Now they can be played with in perpetuity. Of course, that’s not exactly what happens as Andy’s toys discover (almost) too late that the center’s toy leader, Lotso-Huggin’-Bear, voiced by Ned Beatty, runs the place like a prison camp. “Face it, we’re just trash,” says Lotso in a pivotal moment, masking his hurt at being lost by his owner, a girl named Daisy.

Filled with taut, well-edited action sequences—without sacrificing any of the heart and soul of the previous films—Toy Story 3 was a major box office and critical success. It also was a major leap forward technically from the first two films, and even a tiny bit scary as the toy gang faces their “mortality” in the movie’s climax.

Toy Story 3

Watch on Disney+

Watch on Amazon Prime Video

Toy Story 4

Premiered: 2019

Another massive hit with critics and at the box office, Toy Story 4 picks up where the previous installment left off. Woody and the gang are now with Bonnie, a kindergartner who received Andy’s donated toys, but they are struggling to fit into the existing toy hierarchy. In an attempt to protect Bonnie, who is nervous about starting school, Woody tags along in her backpack and meets Forky, a toy Bonnie makes from a discarded plastic utensil.

Forky suffers from anxiety over the fact that he was made from trash, and repeatedly tries to throw himself away. Woody is aghast and keeps rescuing Forky, including during a family RV trip, where the two meet up with Woody’s old pal Bo Peep (voiced by Annie Potts). It turns out Bo has been traveling around as a “lost toy,” free from ownership and very happy about that fact, thank you very much.

Much of the action centers around getting back to the RV (Keanu Reeves shows up to help as Duke Caboom, a 1970s stunt-car toy), complete with detours at an antique shop and a traveling carnival. The familiar angst of Woody (and now Forky) translated into the franchise’s biggest box-office haul to date, grossing more than $1 billion worldwide. But the film switches up the narrative of toys needing to be owned and loved by a specific child: In the end, Woody ends up joining Bo on the road and letting Bonnie, and his past with Andy, go.

Toy Story 4

Watch on Disney+

Watch on Amazon Prime Video

Toy Story 5

Premiered: 2026

Excitement is high for Toy Story 5, which is actually the sixth film in the franchise. (Didn’t see 2022’s Lightyear spin-off? You’re not alone—unfortunately, it was a flop.) The latest installment brings back Hanks, Allen and Cusack as the lead toy trio of Woody, Buzz and Jessie, and it’s even getting a new song by Taylor Swift called “I Knew It, I Knew You,” which will be released on June 5 ahead of the June 19 premiere.

Here’s the premise: It’s been 30 years since the first Toy Story, and a lot has happened in the world of toys and tech. Enter Bonnie’s new “toy,” Lilypad. Voiced by The Morning Show‘s Greta Lee, Lilypad is a modern tablet device that looks like an an innocent frog but, as the official trailer indicates, definitely has a disruptive agenda.

Andy, who was 7 years old in 1995, could now have kids of his own, so sit with that a while as we wait for the release. Potts and Reeves are also returning, along with John Ratzenberger (Hamm), Wallace Shawn (Rex) and Jim Varney (Slinky Dog). Toy Story 5 will be released nationwide on June 19. We can’t wait.
